items.title: Anthropic releases its first Mythos-class model Claude Fable
items.raw_content: Anthropic just announced Claude Fable 5, a new AI model it said is the most powerful model it has ever made widely available. According to the company, Fable 5 "shows exceptional performance in software engineering, knowledge work, and vision," with its lead over other models growing as tasks become longer and more complex. Fable 5 […]

items.title: How an e-scooter founder raised $5 million to build space data centers
items.raw_content: Orbital founder Euwyn Poon built 250,000 scooters at Spin. Now he wants to launch 10,000 space data centers.

items.title: Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today
items.raw_content: Anthropic is releasing Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available to the public. The model comes with guardrails that block responses in high-risk areas like cybersecurity and biology.
items.summary: Anthropic is releasing Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available to the public. The model comes with guardrails that block responses in high-risk areas like cybersecurity and biology.

items.title: OpenAI files for IPO, following Anthropic
items.raw_content: OpenAI on Monday checked off a preliminary step in the IPO race that it and rival Anthropic have been competing in for the better part of a year: The company announced it has confidentially submitted a Form S-1 with the US Securities and Exchange Commission, following Anthropic's decision to do the same on June 1st. items.title: Apple is embracing the fantasy of AI photo editing
items.raw_content: Apple used to question whether generative AI-powered editing features were worth the risk of distorting our perceptions of the world. Now it seems Apple no longer believes that photos should accurately capture reality. At WWDC 2026, the company announced a host of new AI-powered photo editing tools. They give users effortless powers of manipulating images […]

items.raw_content: Apple kicked off its annual developer conference with bold promises about AI. items.title: It’s not FAANG anymore. It’s MANGOS.
items.raw_content: With SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI all eyeing massive public debuts, the tech industry may soon have a new class of corporate overlords — and a new acronym to match. Say goodbye to FAANG and hello to MANGOS.

items.title: Can tech companies learn to love cheaper AI models?
items.raw_content: If those same AI workloads can be handled by cheaper models without affecting quality, it would mean a massive shift in the economics of AI.

items.title: Microsoft AI chief walks back comments about AI taking over white-collar work
items.raw_content: Microsoft AI head Mustafa Suleyman is walking back his statement about AI automating jobs done by white-collar workers, including lawyers, accountants, and project managers. items.title: Apple’s best AI idea looks a lot like vibe coding
items.raw_content: Most of Apple's current AI ideas are roughly the same as everyone else's AI ideas. items.title: Apple’s AI pitch will live or die by its privacy promise
items.raw_content: As expected, yesterday's WWDC keynote was mostly about AI. And also as expected, Apple tried to turn its late arrival into its sales pitch: It didn't rush into AI because it was taking its time to do things right. In this case, "right" means "with more privacy than anyone else." It's a good pitch - […]

items.title: Apple is using AI to fix Safari’s extension problem
items.raw_content: Apple is trying to solve one of Safari's biggest weaknesses with AI. Safari has long lacked the robust library of extensions that its rivals have, mainly due to the stringent development requirements from Apple. But now, Apple is inviting users to essentially vibe-code their own extensions. In a demo shared by Apple, the company showed […]

items.title: Amazon employees ask Seattle to put the brakes on new data centers
items.raw_content: On Tuesday, the Seattle City Council will vote on whether to enact a one-year moratorium on new data centers - just two months after several companies proposed building five large-scale centers in the city. Among the moratorium's fiercest supporters are current employees from the city's biggest tech giant, Amazon, who joined others to testify in […]

items.title: Amazon is launching AI-generated custom merch
items.raw_content: Amazon is expanding its print-on-demand features to AI-generated designs created using Alexa for Shopping for products like T-shirts, water bottles, and hoodies. Shoppers can use text prompts to generate images that are then printed onto blanks for sale on Amazon. They can then share the link to the design so other people can buy the […]
